label_source: Layer-1 shape code cmp,vp,vpc,naive = L,W,L,W: cmplog AND value_profile_cmplog BLOCK; value_profile AND naive RESOLVE. value_profile resolving alongside naive (and vpc blocking alongside cmplog) isolates the discriminator to the I2S stage: anti-synergy where I2SRandReplace hurts BOTH I2S-bearing arms. The independent test must show I2S-anti without re-reading the resolve pattern (G2).

hypotheses.label: I2S substitutes a fixed-offset structural literal (decoy) that pins the loser corpus to the valid-structure path and away from the winner's free-byte target

params.note: decoy = the structural literal I2S copies in (sfnt header 00 01 00 00 + GSUB/GPOS/MATH FOURCCs at offset 0x0c for 5323/5353; Hebrew mark codepoints e.g. 0x05B7/0x05BC for 5784). target = the winner's free leading-byte / fall-through bytes. KEEP VERBATIM (prior-confirmed >=1 branch). NOTE for arbiter: branches 5664/5968/6253 (formerly parked in i2s_no_operand_budget_drain) ALSO score under this rule and were confirmed by it — they are decoy-substitution members in fact, not budget-drain; the prior author's pessimism about their fixed-offset operand was wrong.

hypotheses.prediction: On the I2S-bearing (cmplog/vpc) corpus vs naive corpus, the decoy structural literal is ENRICHED (decoy_enrich > 0) while the winner-target byte is DEPLETED (signed_target_enrich < 0). A pro-I2S 'magic-gate' alternative would predict the opposite (signed_target_enrich > 0, the I2S arm enriches the gate-satisfying byte). A no-effect/random alternative predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0. So a clearly NEGATIVE signed enrichment falsifiably picks anti-decoy-substitution over both.

hypotheses.label: I2S operand substitution collapses corpus composition onto a few CMP-matched byte patterns, inflating/homogenizing the I2S-arm corpus so it cannot build the diverse runtime state (linked-list population) the structural predicate needs

params.note: NEW descriptor. corpus_size_ratio = median saved-corpus size of the I2S arm (cmplog/vpc) / non-I2S arm (naive/value_profile) per trial. composition_entropy_ratio = byte-value Shannon entropy of the I2S arm corpus / non-I2S arm corpus (homogenization => I2S arm collapses onto few values => ratio < 1). openthread HAS an on-disk corpus (data_realities). The repetitive '5c 78 30 30' run cited for 13302 is the homogenization signature. KEEP VERBATIM (prior-confirmed). NOTE: openthread/13300 and 13302 are flagged manual_demote (stateful LinkedList op, decoy_enrich~0.08) — this hypothesis is retained as the standing mechanism for the homogenization subtype; the arbiter honors the manual demotion for those two branches.

hypotheses.prediction: The I2S arm's corpus is inflated AND its byte-composition entropy is collapsed relative to the non-I2S arm (corpus_size_ratio >= 1.3 AND composition_entropy_ratio < 0.85). A 'no homogenization' alternative (I2S merely slower) predicts comparable composition entropy (ratio ~ 1) — so a LOW entropy ratio falsifiably distinguishes homogenization from generic slowness. G2-clean: it reads corpus composition, never the resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: Gate is an EXACT-equality test against a short codepoint literal that the WINNER (non-I2S) corpus lands by free havoc byte mutation, while the I2S stage diverts mutation energy away from the literal so the I2S-arm corpus stalls FARTHER from it in Hamming distance

params.note: KEEP VERBATIM (round-1 decidable). The gate `u == 0x0E0Eu || u == 0x0E0Fu` is a fixed exact value — not a fixed-OFFSET byte literal (the codepoint can appear at any read offset, so operand_enrichment is blind), so value_distance_reached is the right tool: it slides a 2-byte window across each arm's seeds and takes the MIN Hamming distance to the operand. Discriminator here is which ARM lands closer (winner_closer over winner=naive/vp vs loser=cmplog/vpc), NOT vp-vs-cmplog. CAVEAT: width=2 triggers the short-operand chance-alignment flag; we therefore require BOTH a winner-reaches-zero AND a clear gap (not just winner_closer) so chance alignment of the loser doesn't mask the effect. harfbuzz has an on-disk corpus + DB seeds for this rep (data_realities OK).

hypotheses.prediction: The WINNER arm (naive/value_profile) corpus contains a 2-byte window at Hamming distance ~0 to 0x0E0E/0x0E0F (it actually lands the literal), while the LOSER arm (cmplog/vpc) stalls at a strictly larger min distance (distance_gap > 0 with winner_closer=true). This rules out the NULL alternative 'I2S has no effect, both arms equally close' (which predicts distance_gap ~ 0) and the PRO-I2S alternative 'I2S substitutes the codepoint and gets the loser closer' (which predicts winner_closer=false / loser nearer). A genuinely NEGATIVE gap (loser farther) falsifiably isolates I2S-anti energy-diversion away from a free-byte literal. G2-clean: corpus Hamming distance to a literal is independent of the resolve outcome (a corpus can be close yet block, or far yet resolve).
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 AND naive_min_distance <= 0.0

hypotheses.label: I2S (and ctx) anchor the I2S-arm corpus to LARGE valid-header inputs; the branch's true side needs an input shorter than a fixed structural minimum (128-byte cmsICCHeader) that only plain havoc byte-deletion produces, so the I2S arm holds systematically LONGER seeds and never emits the short truncating input

params.note: ROUND-2 FIX (rule #4 cross-server). NEW descriptor corpus_queue:struct_size_ratio:seed_length:per_trial (distinct from joint_necessity, which is vpc-resolving-oriented + needs font tags; here it is a pure median-seed-LENGTH ratio of the I2S arm vs the non-I2S arm, no tokens). median_seed_length_ratio = median saved-seed byte length of the I2S arm (cmplog/vpc) / non-I2S arm (naive/value_profile) per trial. winner_below_minimum = the non-I2S arm has at least one saved seed below the 128-byte header minimum (the truncating input) while the I2S arm has none. lcms is a SERVER-B target: server B owns the lcms corpus and CAN score this. Per rule #4 we do NOT mark this decidable:false merely because s4 lacks the corpus — the owning server arbitrates. The mechanism IS discriminable; only the data lives on another server. data_realities: corpora exist for lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty on sB.

hypotheses.prediction: median_seed_length_ratio >= 1.5 (the I2S/ctx arm holds systematically LONGER, header-anchored seeds) AND winner_below_minimum == true (only the non-I2S arm ever drops below the 128-byte header, producing the Read-underflow truncation that takes the true side). This rules out the NULL 'I2S has no length effect, both arms similar length' (predicts ratio ~ 1, winner_below_minimum either way) and the alternative 'both arms truncate equally, divergence is elsewhere' (predicts winner_below_minimum yet ratio ~ 1). G2-clean: median seed LENGTH and the count of sub-128-byte seeds are read from corpus bytes, never from the branch resolve outcome — a corpus can hold short seeds yet still block, or long seeds yet resolve.

hypotheses.label: Gate is a table-/state-machine-derived, mask/range, or shaper-category value with NO fixed-offset input-byte CMP operand and NO exact value literal; I2S substitution finds nothing to copy and its overhead dilutes the havoc budget that would otherwise land the codepoint/category by random exploration

params.note: ROUND-2 RE-CONFIRMED decidable:false. 5619 = mask/range test (u & ~0xFF)==0x0E00 on a derived Unicode codepoint (byte_signature single_byte, dispersed — the target 0x0e high-byte has no fixed gate offset and the mask means no exact 2-byte literal value_distance_reached could target without single-byte chance-alignment noise). 5989 = USE shaper halant category (use_category()==USE(H)/HVM/IS) assigned by harfbuzz machine-class TABLES from glyph properties — no input-byte literal at all. Neither has a fixed-offset literal (operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature) NOR an exact value literal value_distance_reached could target. The round-2 pivot candidates (depth_reach, joint_necessity, token_count) do NOT rescue these: depth_reach is branch-line scoped, so at line 46 (5619) / 357 (5989) it would only show winner-reaches/loser-doesn't = the resolve pattern restated (G2 violation); joint_necessity/token_count target structural-assembly size, which is not what differentiates a havoc-budget-drain on a mask/table gate. There is no POSITIVE, G2-independent corpus signature of 'budget drain' — only the ABSENCE of an operand signal — so per G3 we do NOT assert a mechanism on absence-of-evidence.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature OR signed_target_enrich ~ 0; no built measurement (including the round-2 pivots depth_reach / joint_necessity / token_count) yields a POSITIVE, G2-independent signature that distinguishes 'budget drain on a non-operand gate' from the null/underpowered case. Honest inconclusive.

hypotheses.label: Branch carries no comparison operand (presence/non-null gate reached by generic table/loop traversal); the I2S stage's operand substitution diverts the loser into an EXPENSIVE SIBLING subtree (deep GPOS sanitize/coverage paths) the winner never enters, so the loser never reaches the gate

params.note: ROUND-2 RE-CONFIRMED decidable:false. The distinguishing evidence (card 5576: loser-ONLY invocation of GPOSProxy::GPOSProxy 0-vs-189, PairPosFormat1_3::sanitize 0-vs-49, Mark*PosFormat1_2 sanitize, all W=0/L>0) is the execution count of SIBLING diversion functions, not of the branch line itself. The round-2 pivot depth_reach is BRANCH-LINE scoped: at line 1989 it would only show winner-reaches/loser-doesn't, which is the resolve pattern restated (G2 violation), NOT the diversion signature. No BUILT tool measures per-function sibling-subtree execution divergence between W and L seed sets (event_cooccurrence here would be a per-FUNCTION exec-count descriptor that does not exist; the data_realities note that seed_lineage event_cooccurrence on an I2S event is also infeasible). Building such a descriptor is feasible in principle but speculative for a single branch; per G3 we keep this honestly inconclusive rather than invent the measurement.

hypotheses.prediction: Would predict loser-blocking seeds invoke the deep GPOS sanitize functions (PairPosFormat1_3::sanitize etc.) at high count while winner-resolving seeds invoke them ~0 — but NO built tool measures sibling-subtree invocation, and the only built reach tool (depth_reach) reads the branch line itself (= resolve pattern, G2-fail). UNMEASURABLE with current tools. Falls to inconclusive.

notes:
ROUND-2 RE-DESIGN (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET/MONOTONIC: all three prior-confirmed/decidable hypotheses are carried VERBATIM — i2s_decoy_substitution_overfit (signed_target_enrich<-0.3), i2s_homogenizes_corpus_starves_state_diversity (corpus_size_ratio>=1.3 AND composition_entropy_ratio<0.85), and i2s_diverts_from_exact_codepoint_literal (value_distance winner_closer + gap + naive reaches 0) — so the 6 confirmed decoy branches, the homogenization subtype, and the 5958 codepoint-diversion branch all re-validate unchanged.

ROUND-2 TARGET = the 4 rule_not_met branches: lcms/2149, harfbuzz/5576, 5619, 5989 (openthread/13300+13302 are manual_demote and left for the arbiter under the standing homogenization hypothesis).

PROMOTED TO DECIDABLE (1): i2s_anchors_large_valid_blocks_truncation for lcms/2149. Round 1 wrongly marked this decidable:false because lcms has no corpus on s4. Per re-design rule #4 (cross-server), corpus absence on the RUNNING server is NOT a mechanism gap — lcms is a server-B target and server B owns the corpus. So this round gives it a REAL falsifiable rule over a NEW descriptor corpus_queue:struct_size_ratio:seed_length:per_trial (median seed-LENGTH ratio of the I2S/ctx arm vs the non-I2S arm + a 'winner has a sub-128-byte seed' flag). The mechanism (I2S/ctx anchor LONGER header-valid seeds; the true side needs sub-128-byte truncation only havoc byte-deletion makes) is genuinely discriminable and G2-independent (reads seed bytes, not the resolve outcome). It is decidable:true; the OWNING server (B) arbitrates it. This is a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism from decoy-substitution (no fixed-offset literal), from value-distance (no value literal — it is a length/truncation gate), and from homogenization (length, not byte-composition entropy).

HONESTLY decidable:false (G3) — RE-CONFIRMED after testing the round-2 pivot tools: (a) i2s_no_operand_budget_drain holds 5619 (mask/range on derived codepoint, dispersed single byte, no fixed offset and no exact 2-byte literal) and 5989 (USE shaper TABLE-derived halant category, no input literal at all). The pivots do not rescue them: depth_reach is branch-line scoped (restates resolve = G2-fail); joint_necessity/token_count measure structural-assembly size, irrelevant to a havoc-budget-drain on a mask/table gate. No positive, independent signature exists. (b) i2s_off_path_subtree_diversion (harfbuzz/5576): the discriminating evidence is loser-only invocation of deep GPOS sanitize SIBLING functions (W=0/L>0) — sibling-subtree execution divergence, which NO built tool measures; depth_reach reads the branch line itself (=resolve, G2-fail). Kept as a NAMED honest-inconclusive distinct from generic budget-drain, rather than inventing a speculative single-branch per-function-exec descriptor.

WHY THE PIVOTS DID NOT OPEN MORE LABELS: the brief flagged joint_necessity/struct_size_and_token_count, depth_reach, token_count as untried. joint_necessity + token_count are assembly-DEPTH/size tools for the JOINT (vpc-needs-both) shape; this is an I2S-ANTI shape (vpc BLOCKS), so structural-assembly size is not the discriminator for 5619/5989/5576. depth_reach is the one reach tool but it is branch-line scoped and every anti branch trivially has winner-reaches/loser-doesn't at the line, so using it here would re-read the resolve pattern (G2). Only the seed-LENGTH facet of struct_size_ratio (a new, narrower descriptor) was genuinely applicable, and it applied to the lcms truncation gate — promoted above.

data_realities respected: no seed_lineage 'I2S event' (I2S untagged); no byte-alignment on different-sized structural seeds; value_distance_reached + operand_enrichment scoped to harfbuzz (on-disk corpus + DB seeds); lcms seed-length test scoped to server B (corpus owner); value_profile treated as a feedback (its effect read via corpus length / value-distance / composition, never a lineage gradient event).
